nodes.label: The System Monitor
nodes.rationale: A dark, tactile environment where code, status, and telemetry are primary citizens. Rejects 'AI slop' aesthetics in favor of rigid grids and terminal prompts.

nodes.label: Terminal-First Aesthetic
nodes.rationale: Monospaced typography and command-line affordances drive the interface. Uses prefixes like ❯, ./, or // to indicate command intent.

nodes.label: Tonal Layering
nodes.rationale: Depth is conveyed through background shifts and border treatments instead of drop shadows. Surfaces move from Obsidian base to Layer 1 and Layer 2.

nodes.label: High-Craft Design
nodes.rationale: Obsessive attention to detail in animations, telemetry data, and system-level status.

nodes.label: Show, Don't Tell Principle
nodes.rationale: Use live telemetry and working code patterns (typewriters, terminal logs) to prove expertise rather than static claims.

nodes.label: The Machine Aesthetic
nodes.rationale: Embrace the raw materials of computing—grids, terminal prompts, and status daemons.
